_meta.description: Flat process activity registry for the IMHOGEN ERM. Each entry is one atomic step in the QMS workflow. Use pid to cross-reference tool_instances; use documents_used/generated for dependency graph construction.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive automatic notification of a new client inquiry via website form or email.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the notification contains readable text and contact info.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Email filtering to spam; missed notifications.

process_activities.activity_description: Read the inquiry to perform a preliminary spam/relevance filter.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the request broadly aligns with IMHOGEN's engineering services.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Wasting time on spam or out-of-scope solicitations.

process_activities.activity_description: Create a new project entry in the tracker and copy/paste all client details into the respective fields.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Double-check that phone numbers and email addresses are copied without typos.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Typos in contact info leading to lost leads.

process_activities.activity_description: Send standard acknowledgment email to the client stating the inquiry was received and outlining the next step.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the client's name is spelled correctly in the template.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client feels ignored if acknowledgment is delayed.

process_activities.activity_description: Contact the client to propose 2-3 available time slots for a technical discovery meeting.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure proposed times fall within standard operating hours.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Scheduling conflicts causing delays.

process_activities.activity_description: Generate a calendar invite with a video conferencing link and a standard meeting agenda, and send it to the client.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the video link works and the time zone is correct.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Technical difficulties joining the meeting.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct the discovery meeting to extract exact technical needs, operational constraints, and expected outcomes.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the client explicitly states their budget constraints and timelines.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Failing to ask critical constraint questions.

process_activities.activity_description: Clean up raw meeting notes and upload them into the central documentation repository.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all technical jargon and constraints are accurately transcribed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Loss of critical technical details post-meeting.

process_activities.activity_description: Share the Needs Assessment Notes with the Engineering and R&D team for internal review.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm receipt of the document by the Engineering team.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Bottlenecks if the engineering team misses the notification.

process_activities.activity_description: Evaluate if the department has the required CAD software, tooling, and skills to execute the client's request.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Cross-check required software (e.g., Inventor) against current licenses.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Committing to a project without the necessary software/skills.

process_activities.activity_description: Evaluate the request for regulatory, safety, and operational risks.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all physical safety standards related to the request are flagged.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Accepting a project with severe liability risks.

process_activities.activity_description: Compile the Capability and Risk assessments into a final Go/No-Go Feasibility Report.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Final managerial review of the Go/No-Go recommendation.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Incorrect Go/No-Go decision based on flawed data.

process_activities.activity_description: If "Go", write out the exact technical deliverables (CAD models, BOMs, reports) the department will provide.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure deliverables are strictly defined to prevent scope creep.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Vague deliverables leading to client disputes later.

process_activities.activity_description: Estimate the required engineering man-hours, software usage time, and any specialized resource costs.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify hourly rates match the current department standards.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Underpricing the engineering effort.

process_activities.activity_description: Combine the Scope of Work and Cost Estimation into the formal Project Brief & Draft Budget document.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Check formatting, spelling, and mathematical accuracy of the budget.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Presenting an unprofessional or mathematically incorrect brief.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a brief internal meeting to formally hand over the Project Brief to the contract administration team.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Both parties must verbally agree that the scope is clear enough for contracting.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Legal drafting begins with misunderstood technical parameters.

process_activities.activity_description: Change the project status in the central database from "Onboarding" to "Contracting Phase".
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the status change is reflected across all dashboards.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Tracking confusion if the system still shows "Onboarding".

process_activities.activity_description: Archive all onboarding raw documents (emails, initial notes) into the project's permanent digital folder.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all files are correctly named and tagged before archiving.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Cluttered or lost historical files.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the formal Project Brief & Draft Budget from the Onboarding handoff and review for clarity.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all technical deliverables and budget figures are explicitly stated.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Ambiguous inputs leading to poorly drafted contracts.

process_activities.activity_description: Access the central legal repository and download the appropriate standard contract templates (SLA, NDA, SOW).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the templates are the most currently approved versions by legal.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Using outdated legal templates with voided clauses.

process_activities.activity_description: Populate the selected templates with client details, scope of work, budget, and specific constraints.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Double-check client legal name, addresses, and monetary figures.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Typos in critical financial or legal identifying information.

process_activities.activity_description: Route the Initial Draft Contract to the Project Manager to verify that the technical scope matches the department's capability.
process_activities.quality_control_point: PM must sign off that the timeline and technical deliverables are realistic.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Legal commitment to impossible engineering timelines.

process_activities.activity_description: Route the PM Reviewed Draft to the Finance Team to verify payment milestones, taxes, and total contract value.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Finance must verify the payment schedule aligns with cash flow needs.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unfavorable payment terms that restrict project funding.

process_activities.activity_description: Submit the draft to the Legal Advisor (if applicable) for a final compliance and liability check.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Check for excessive liability, indemnification, or IP forfeiture clauses.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Exposing IMHOGEN to severe legal or financial liabilities.

process_activities.activity_description: Draft a formal email and send the Internally Approved Draft to the client for their review.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the email clearly states the deadline for their review/redlines.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Open-ended reviews causing project scheduling delays.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive client feedback/redlines. If changes are requested, schedule a negotiation meeting.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure relevant decision-makers (Head of Ops, PM) are invited.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Inability to resolve contract disputes via email alone.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct the negotiation meeting, discuss disputed clauses, and agree on mutual compromises.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Never agree to technical or financial changes without PM/Finance input.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Verbal agreement to terms that violate department policies.

process_activities.activity_description: Update the contract document based on the agreed compromises and conduct a final internal read-through.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure no unintended clauses were altered during the redline process.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Sneaky or accidental changes inserted into the final text.

process_activities.activity_description: Upload the Finalized Contract into the digital signature platform and tag the required signature fields.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the correct authorized signers for both IMHOGEN and the client.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Contract sent to an unauthorized representative.

process_activities.activity_description: Dispatch the digital envelope to the client and monitor the platform for completion.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Send automatic 48-hour reminders if the document remains unsigned.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Project stalling due to forgotten signatures.

process_activities.activity_description: Once signed by all parties, download the final document and its digital audit trail certificate.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the audit certificate is attached to prove legal validity.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Inability to prove the signature's authenticity in a dispute.

process_activities.activity_description: Upload the Executed Contract into the central secure legal repository and apply appropriate metadata tags.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure strict access control (read-only) is applied to the final file.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unauthorized alteration or deletion of executed contracts.

process_activities.activity_description: Update the CRM/Project Tracker status to "Contract Executed" and trigger a notification to the Finance Team to begin Payment Scheduling.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm the Finance Team acknowledges the trigger to start billing.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Failure to initiate invoicing after the contract is signed.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Phase Initiation Notice" and the Executed Contract from the Business Operations Team.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm receipt of the fully executed contract before initiating any financial setup.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Setting up billing on an unfinalized or unsigned contract.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Read the contract's commercial terms to extract exact milestone descriptions, payment percentages, and due dates.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the extracted milestones exactly match the contract's language.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Misinterpreting payment terms leading to incorrect billing schedules.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Create a new client profile and project ledger in the accounting system, inputting the extracted payment schedule.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Finance Manager must perform a spot-check on the entered schedule vs the contract.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Data entry errors causing missed or incorrect future invoices.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Receive formal notification and proof of completion for a specific project milestone from the Project Manager.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the PM's proof of completion aligns with the contract's definition of the milestone.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Billing a client before the engineering value has actually been delivered.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Generate a draft invoice in the accounting software corresponding to the achieved milestone and scheduled amount.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure PO numbers, tax IDs, and bank details are clearly visible on the invoice.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Invoices rejected by the client's accounts payable due to missing details.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Review and approve the draft invoice for mathematical accuracy and tax compliance before issuance.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Dual-authorization required for invoices exceeding a predefined monetary threshold.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Sending mathematically incorrect invoices that damage company credibility.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Draft a formal email, attach the Approved Invoice (PDF format), and transmit it to the client's designated billing contact.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the email address is the official accounts payable contact, not just the technical lead.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Invoice sitting unread in the wrong person's inbox.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Log into the corporate banking portal daily to monitor and download reports of incoming client deposits.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure secure network connections and VPNs are used when accessing banking portals.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unnoticed funds leading to unnecessary project delays.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Cross-reference incoming bank deposits against outstanding invoices in the accounting software.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Match the exact deposited amount and payment reference number to the specific invoice.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Misallocating funds to the wrong client or project ledger.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Generate a formal Payment Receipt and email it to the client to acknowledge successful receipt of funds.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Issue the receipt within 24 hours of the funds clearing the bank.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client disputes or uncertainty regarding their account standing.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Change the invoice status to "Paid" and send a "Payment Cleared - Proceed" notification to the Project Manager.
process_activities.quality_control_point: PM must acknowledge the unblock notice before deploying engineering resources.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Engineering team remaining idle because they are unaware funds have cleared.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Generate an Accounts Receivable (AR) aging report weekly to identify invoices that have crossed their payment grace period.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the report accurately reflects the net terms (e.g., Net 30) agreed in the contract.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Accumulation of bad debt and severe cash flow bottlenecks.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Send a polite, standardized follow-up email to clients with overdue invoices, requesting payment status.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Maintain a professional tone to preserve the client relationship while being firm on terms.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Damaging client relations with aggressive or premature collection tactics.

process_activities.workbook_name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.
process_activities.activity_description: If payment is delayed beyond critical thresholds, issue a formal "Stop Work" notice to the Project Manager.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Escalate to Head of Business Operations before halting major engineering work.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Continuing to burn man-hours and resources on a defaulting client.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Phase Initiation Notice" and the finalized Project Brief from the Business Operations Team.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the Project Brief contains explicitly approved timelines and budgets.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Initiating planning based on unapproved or draft requirements.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Read the Project Brief to list the specific technical competencies, software platforms (e.g., Inventor), and estimated man-hours required.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all required niche skills or specialized software modules are identified.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Realizing midway through the project that the team lacks a critical skill.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Populate the standard Resource Request Form with the required roles, hours, and duration, and submit it to HR/Engineering Management.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Double-check that the requested man-hours align with the approved budget.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Requesting more man-hours than the project budget can afford.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Receive the Resource Request Form and open the department's scheduling dashboard to view current staff workloads.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure no single engineer is assigned a sustained workload exceeding 85% capacity.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Staff burnout, missed deadlines, and poor quality output due to overloading.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Cross-reference the requested competencies with the skills matrix of available engineers, draftsmen, and QA/QC staff.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Match the complexity of the project with the seniority level of the assigned engineer.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assigning a junior engineer to a high-risk, complex system design without proper support.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Select the specific personnel for the project and formally log their assigned hours and roles into the tracking system.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that planned leave or PTO does not conflict with critical project milestones.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Key personnel going on vacation during a critical design deadline.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Receive the Team Assignment Record and trigger the IT/Admin provisioning workflow.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure only the individuals listed on the Assignment Record receive access.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Providing confidential client data access to unauthorized personnel.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Create dedicated project folders in the central Document Management System and CAD PDM Vault, applying strict access controls.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Apply standardized naming conventions to all newly created digital folders.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Chaotic file storage leading to lost CAD models or overwritten documents.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Set up dedicated internal communication channels (e.g., specific chat groups or email threads) and invite the assigned team.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the Project Manager is granted "Admin" rights over the new communication channels.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Fragmented communication happening outside the official project channels.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Draft a structured kick-off presentation summarizing the client's goals, the technical constraints, the budget, and the timeline.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all proprietary client details are masked if the presentation is stored broadly.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Team members lacking a holistic understanding of the project's 'why'.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Send a calendar invite for the internal kick-off meeting to the assigned team and attach the Project Brief for pre-reading.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the scheduled time works for all assigned critical personnel.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Key technical experts missing the foundational alignment meeting.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Conduct the video/in-person kick-off meeting, explicitly outlining the roles, expectations, and the "black-box" input/output deliverables.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Require verbal confirmation from the Lead Engineer that the timeline is understood.
process_activities.risk_considerations: The technical team leaving the meeting with misaligned expectations.

process_activities.workbook_name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.
process_activities.activity_description: Log into the Project Tracking Software, formally lock the baseline schedule/budget, and change the project status to "Engineering Design Phase".
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the baseline matches the kick-off agreements exactly; restrict editing access.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unapproved changes creeping into the schedule after the kick-off.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Establish rough factors for all life phases (technology, operators, inputs, outputs) and analyze the TP environment.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify all input/output operands match the Project Brief constraints.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Missing a critical operational environment constraint early on.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Quantify requirements, assign tolerances, and allocate properties across functional life phases and operators.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Validate tolerances against available standard workshop capabilities.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Over-tolerancing leading to unmanufacturable or costly designs.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Compile, review, and lock the formal, multi-dimensional design specifications.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Formal sign-off on the Pr-Set before any design work begins.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Proceeding to conceptual design with ambiguous or conflicting specifications.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Analyze the technical process viewpoints, assess difficulty, and assign specific design tasks to operators.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure assigned engineers have the required technical competency.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assigning complex datum calculations to junior staff.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Estimate design costs, map the schedule, and set up phase progress tracking mechanisms.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Baseline schedule must align with overall project budget and timeline.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Underestimating required man-hours for complex FEA or CAD modeling.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Establish the operands (Material, Energy, Information) and the technological principles of the operations process.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify logical flow: all input operands must logically convert to outputs.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Incomplete transformation mapping leading to missing mechanical functions.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Distribute effects between technical/human systems, defining main, assisting, and evoked functions.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure no critical load-bearing or safety function is overlooked.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Functional black-boxes left unresolved before structural synthesis.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Populate the morphological matrix with action principles and combine function carriers (organs) into unified concepts.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure at least 3 distinct conceptual variants are generated.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Fixation on a single design path without exploring optimal alternatives.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Establish spatial topologies (Iconic/Principle) and evaluate proposals to select the optimal Final Organ Structure.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Justify the selected organ structure mathematically against the Pr-Set.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Selecting a suboptimal layout due to bias rather than technical merit.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Establish preliminary layouts, rough forms, and define constructional design groups from the organ structure.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Clash detection and clearance check at the preliminary layout stage.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Components interfering spatially during assembly.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Definitively establish dimensions, material treatments, part decompositions, and perform strength analyses.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Tolerance stack-up analysis and FEA validation against the Pr-Set.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Structural failure or excessive deflection under operational loads.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Generate individual detailed manufacturing drawings, control assembly drawings, and hierarchical part lists.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Drawing checker review (Title blocks, GD&T, notes) by Lead Engineer.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Releasing incomplete or inaccurate drawings to the fabrication floor.

process_activities.workbook_name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)
process_activities.activity_description: Prepare manufacturing instructions, conduct a final design audit, and release the complete technical data package.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Complete sign-off matrix verification prior to manufacturing release.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Manufacturing unapproved or obsolete file versions.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Approved Technical Data Package" (CAD, Drawings, BOM, Reports) from the Lead Engineer.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify all files possess the required internal QA/QC signature before sending to the client.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Submitting unverified or draft engineering files to the client.

process_activities.activity_description: Upload the deliverables into a secure, client-facing digital portal or package them into a secure file-transfer link.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure access permissions are strictly limited to the authorized client contacts.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unauthorized interception of proprietary IMHOGEN designs.

process_activities.activity_description: Draft and send a calendar invite for a formal "Deliverable Presentation Meeting" to walk the client through the design.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Schedule the meeting within 48 hours of sending the deliverables to maintain momentum.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client reviewing complex technical files without proper context, leading to confusion.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct the presentation meeting, using 3D viewers to explain technical decisions, constraints, and functional compliance.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the Lead Engineer explains why certain design constraints were enforced.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client rejecting a design because they do not understand the underlying engineering logic.

process_activities.activity_description: Send the standardized "Client Feedback Form" to the client immediately following the presentation.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Formally state the deadline (e.g., 5 business days) for submitting the feedback form.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Open-ended feedback timelines stalling the project indefinitely.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the completed feedback form from the client and extract all requested changes into the central Revision Log.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure every single client comment is captured line-by-line without summarization.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Losing or ignoring a specific client request, leading to dissatisfaction.

process_activities.activity_description: Review the Master Revision Log against the original signed Contract/Project Brief to classify changes as "Minor" or "Major".
process_activities.quality_control_point: "Major" changes (out of scope) must immediately trigger a financial Change Order request.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Performing out-of-scope engineering redesigns for free (Scope Creep).

process_activities.activity_description: Route approved "Minor" revisions back to the engineering team, assigning specific PID tasks for CAD/Drawing updates.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the revision tickets explicitly link back to the specific client feedback line item.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Engineers misunderstanding the requested change and altering the wrong component.

process_activities.activity_description: Execute the requested changes in the CAD models and update the associated 2D drawings and BOMs.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Save the revised files as a new version (e.g., V1.1) to preserve the original baseline.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Overwriting the original design files and losing previous work.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform a QA/QC check on the revised files to ensure the specific feedback was addressed without breaking other system functions.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the new changes still comply with the original Pr-Set safety and load requirements.
process_activities.risk_considerations: A minor revision inadvertently causing a critical structural or functional failure.

process_activities.activity_description: Generate the formal "Delivery & Acceptance Form" stating that all design requirements and revisions have been met.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Cross-check the final deliverable list on the form against the original contract.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Missing a contracted deliverable on the final acceptance sheet.

process_activities.activity_description: Upload the Acceptance Form and the Final Revised Technical Data Package into the e-signature platform.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the digital envelope is sent to the client's authorized legal/financial signatory.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Obtaining a sign-off from an unauthorized client representative.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a brief final Handover Meeting (if requested by the client) to officially close the design phase and answer final questions.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm verbally during the meeting that no further engineering changes will be made.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client attempting to request more changes after the handover meeting.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the executed e-signature, archive all final documents, and trigger the Finance Team to issue the final invoice.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all finalized CAD models are locked in the PDM vault to prevent further editing.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Accidental alterations to the finalized, client-approved engineering files.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Project Phase Closure Notice" and the finalized, QA-approved Bill of Materials (BOM) from the Project Manager.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the BOM has the formal "Released for Manufacturing" digital signature.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Procuring materials based on an obsolete or unapproved draft BOM.

process_activities.activity_description: Import the digital BOM into the procurement dashboard and categorize items into raw materials, standard hardware, and custom components.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure units of measure (e.g., mm, meters, kg) are correctly mapped during import.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unit conversion errors leading to massive over/under ordering of steel sections.

process_activities.activity_description: Cross-reference the Categorized Procurement List against the locked financial budget established in the Project Brief.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Flag any item where the current market price exceeds the estimated baseline budget by >10%.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Material price fluctuations destroying the project's profit margin.

process_activities.activity_description: Generate a Request for Quotation (RFQ) for required materials, specifying exact tolerances, grades, and quantities.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure RFQs for standard structural steel clearly state acceptable tolerance limits for local market variations.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Vendors quoting for sub-standard or incorrect material grades.

process_activities.activity_description: Dispatch RFQs to approved local suppliers (e.g., Suame/Kumasi market vendors) and specialized international distributors via email.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify vendor email addresses and set a strict deadline (e.g., 48 hours) for quote returns.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Project delays due to endlessly waiting on vendor quotes.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive quotes, compile a comparative vendor matrix (price, lead time, quality history), and select the optimal suppliers.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Justify selection if the cheapest vendor is not chosen (e.g., due to faster lead time or better quality).
process_activities.risk_considerations: Selecting an unreliable vendor solely because they offered the lowest price.

process_activities.activity_description: Generate formal Purchase Orders (POs) in the accounting system for the selected vendors, attaching the required engineering specs.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Double-check payment terms, delivery address, and required delivery dates on the PO.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Materials shipped to the wrong address or on the wrong timeline.

process_activities.activity_description: Route the Draft PO to the Finance Manager for final financial authorization and fund allocation.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Finance Manager must verify that sufficient cash flow exists to clear the PO upon delivery.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Issuing POs without the capital to pay the vendor, damaging IMHOGEN's credit.

process_activities.activity_description: Transmit the Approved PO to the vendor and obtain a formal acknowledgment and confirmed delivery schedule.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Do not mark the item as "Ordered" until the vendor explicitly confirms receipt and capacity.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assuming an order is placed when the vendor never actually saw the PO.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the physical delivery at the workshop. Cross-reference the vendor's packing slip against the original PO.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify physical box counts and part numbers before the delivery driver leaves the premises.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Signing for missing boxes and losing leverage to claim missing items.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform incoming Quality Control (QC) on the raw materials using measuring tools (calipers, micrometers) to verify dimensions against the BOM.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Mandate a dimensional and visual check for warping, rust, or incorrect thickness on structural steel.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Accepting sub-standard, out-of-tolerance steel that will ruin the welding jig's accuracy.

process_activities.activity_description: If materials pass QC, formally log them into the digital inventory system and assign them to the specific project storage zone.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Apply physical project tags/labels to the materials to prevent them from being used on other jobs.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Usable project materials being cannibalized by other workshop teams.

process_activities.activity_description: Trigger a notification to the Fabrication Supervisor that all required materials are in stock, QA-approved, and ready for production.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Supervisor must acknowledge the notice to trigger the Fabrication timeline.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Fabrication team sitting idle while materials gather dust in the storage zone.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Fabrication Readiness Notice", Final BOM, and 2D Engineering Drawings from Procurement and Engineering.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all 2D drawings have the correct "Released for Manufacturing" stamp and GD&T notes.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Fabricating from obsolete or unapproved drawing versions.

process_activities.activity_description: Review the 2D drawings and BOM to sequence the manufacturing operations (e.g., rough cut → machine → form → join → finish).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the sequence logically prevents impossible tool-access or assembly situations later in the build.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assembling components out of order, blocking access for final fastening or machining.

process_activities.activity_description: Identify, reserve, and calibrate all required workshop machinery, CNC centers, fixtures, and hand tools needed for the job.
process_activities.quality_control_point: QA/QC must verify that measuring tapes, calipers, and gauges are properly calibrated.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Dimensional errors caused by using uncalibrated or worn-out measuring tools.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a mandatory "Toolbox Talk" to brief the shop floor team on the exact structural goals, GD&T tolerances, and specific process hazards.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Require machine operators and fabricators to verbally confirm their understanding of the critical datum planes.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Floor workers misunderstanding the drawing symbols or safety risks.

process_activities.activity_description: Issue the necessary Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) tailored to the specific fabrication processes (e.g., eye protection, respirators, hearing protection).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Deny workshop floor access to any operator lacking complete, functional PPE.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Severe workplace injuries due to lack of appropriate process protection.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform initial shaping, cutting, or blanking of raw materials to the exact preliminary dimensions specified in the BOM/Cut List.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the dimensions of the first piece before processing the rest of the batch (First-Article Inspection).
process_activities.risk_considerations: Wasting expensive raw materials due to an incorrect initial machine setup or measurement.

process_activities.activity_description: Deburr, chamfer, and clean the edges/surfaces of all prepped materials to prepare them for safe handling and high-integrity joining or final machining.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Visually inspect surfaces to ensure no burrs, oil, or scale remains that could interfere with alignment.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Poor joint integrity or machine tool damage caused by contaminated/burred base materials.

process_activities.activity_description: Load the prepped materials into the designated fixtures, jigs, or machine beds, aligning them strictly to the primary datum planes.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all locating pins and clamps are fully seated before initiating the primary manufacturing process.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Manufacturing a skewed part because the material was not fully seated in the fixture.

process_activities.activity_description: Execute preliminary forming, temporary fastening, or rough machining passes to establish the baseline geometry.
process_activities.quality_control_point: QA/QC must perform a quick squareness and dimensional check before final, irreversible joining/machining begins.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Discovering a dimensional error after the entire assembly is permanently joined or finish-machined.

process_activities.activity_description: Execute the final manufacturing sequence (e.g., final joining, structural fastening, or finishing machine passes) utilizing methods to evenly distribute stress and prevent distortion.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Strictly enforce the defined process sequence to mitigate mechanical or thermal distortion across the structure.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Severe structural distortion (warping) rendering the assembly unusable and out-of-tolerance.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform visual, dimensional, and (if required) Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) on the structural joints and critical machined surfaces.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Flag any defective joint or out-of-tolerance surface for immediate rework before moving to the next stage.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Micro-fractures or weak connections failing under operational load.

process_activities.activity_description: Execute final finishing operations (e.g., grinding flush, polishing, edge routing) to meet the aesthetic and functional surface requirements.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure finishing operations do not reduce material thickness or alter dimensions below the safe minimum limits.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Weakening the structural integrity or altering critical tolerances by over-finishing.

process_activities.activity_description: Apply standard environmental protections (e.g., primer, paint, anodizing, or anti-rust oil) as dictated by the engineering specifications.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure coatings are applied evenly and do not obstruct any threaded holes, sliding rails, or moving joints.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Premature degradation or coating interference with dynamic mechanical assemblies.

process_activities.activity_description: Move the Coated Final Product to the designated testing staging area and update the project tracker to "Ready for Final QA Testing".
process_activities.quality_control_point: Supervisor must digitally sign off that all fabrication steps on the Job Process Sheet were completed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Handing over an unfinished, unchecked physical unit to the final testing phase.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Fabrication Completion Notice" and physically transfer the fabricated structure to the QA/QC staging area.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the structure is securely staged and safe to inspect before beginning work.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Handling damage occurring during transfer to the QA area.

process_activities.activity_description: Retrieve the locked Design Specification (Pr-Set), 2D Drawings, and the original Project Brief from the digital vault.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the retrieved drawings are the exact versions used by the fabrication team.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Inspecting a physical part against an outdated or incorrect digital drawing.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform a comprehensive visual sweep of the structure, checking for sharp edges, burrs, spatter, and coating uniformity.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Flag any exposed, uncoated raw steel that violates the environmental protection specs.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Premature corrosion or operator injury due to missed burrs/sharp edges.

process_activities.activity_description: Measure all critical datums, hole centers, and overall bounding dimensions against the defined GD&T tolerances (e.g., ±1.0mm).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure measuring tools are zeroed and calibrated before taking the first measurement.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Falsely passing an out-of-tolerance dimension due to an uncalibrated caliper.

process_activities.activity_description: Prepare the structure for physical testing by securely anchoring it and gathering test operands (e.g., inserting sample pipes into the welding jig).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure all anchors are tight before applying any dynamic or static loads.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Structure tipping or collapsing during the load test, causing severe injury.

process_activities.activity_description: Actuate all moving parts, clamps, and mechanisms to verify smooth operation, clamping force, and structural deflection under load (e.g., ≤ 0.5 mm).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the structure returns to absolute zero (no permanent deformation) after the load is removed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Plastic deformation indicating a fundamental structural design or material failure.

process_activities.activity_description: If any dimension or test fails, immediately tag the physical structure with a red "DO NOT USE" marker and log the defect.
process_activities.quality_control_point: The NCR must cite the specific drawing coordinate or Pr-Set line item that failed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Ambiguous defect reporting leading to the wrong area being reworked.

process_activities.activity_description: Route the NCR back to the Fabrication Supervisor and Lead Engineer to determine if the part must be reworked, repaired, or scrapped.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Do not authorize a "repair" if the structural integrity has been fundamentally compromised.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Attempting to weld over a critical failure, hiding a fatal flaw from the client.

process_activities.activity_description: Once all tests pass (or rework is successfully re-tested), compile all inspection logs into the final validation package.
process_activities.quality_control_point: The Lead Engineer must countersign the QA Certificate to accept ultimate technical liability.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Releasing a product solely on a technician's signature without engineering oversight.

process_activities.activity_description: Attach the physical QA Release Certificate to the structure and update the digital project status to "Ready for Delivery/Handover".
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the Finance Team is copied on the notice to prepare the final invoice.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Product sitting in the staging area indefinitely because the delivery team was not notified.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Final QA Release Certificate" and interview the Fabrication Supervisor for any on-the-fly workshop modifications made during the build.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure every authorized rework or dimensional shift is captured from the QA reports.
process_activities.risk_considerations: "As-built" physical product not matching the final delivered digital CAD files.

process_activities.activity_description: Update the 3D CAD models, 2D Drawings, and BOM to reflect the exact "As-Built" physical reality of the approved structure.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Increment the file revision number (e.g., Rev A to Rev B) to indicate the final As-Built status.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Overwriting original design intent data without a traceable version history.

process_activities.activity_description: Draft the User Manual, detailing safe operational procedures, ergonomic handling limits, and visual cues for correct use.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the manual addresses all Human System constraints defined in Pr5 (Safe handling, low skill requirement).
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client operators injuring themselves due to unclear usage instructions.

process_activities.activity_description: Draft the Maintenance & Adjustment Guide, specifying lubrication points, replaceable wear element part numbers, and calibration steps.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Include specific tolerance limits (e.g., maximum wear limits before part replacement is required).
process_activities.risk_considerations: Premature equipment failure due to lack of defined preventative maintenance.

process_activities.activity_description: Compile the As-Built Drawings, BOM, User Manual, Maintenance Guide, and QA Certificates into a single, cohesive Technical Data Package (TDP).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the document title blocks, page numbers, and IMHOGEN branding are consistent across the package.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Delivering a disorganized, unprofessional stack of unlinked documents to the client.

process_activities.activity_description: Route the compiled Final TDP to the Project Manager and Lead Engineer for a final administrative and technical sign-off.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Both the PM and Lead Engineer must digitally sign the master cover page of the TDP.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Releasing unverified instructional documents containing technical errors.

process_activities.activity_description: Move all finalized native CAD files, FEA simulation data, and internal emails into the project's permanent "Closed/Archived" digital folder.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Apply strict "Read-Only" permissions to the archived folder for all staff except IT Admins.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Accidental deletion or unauthorized alteration of closed project data.

process_activities.activity_description: Change the state of all associated CAD files within the PDM software to "Released / Locked" to prevent further parametric editing.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify that the PDM system registers the lock and severs check-out privileges for design staff.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Engineers accidentally opening and modifying archived CAD files for new projects.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a brief project post-mortem to extract key technical lessons, reusable CAD sub-assemblies, and standard parts for the internal library.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Strip all confidential client IP from CAD assemblies before adding them to the general IMHOGEN parts library.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Violating client NDAs by reusing proprietary geometries in future public designs.

process_activities.activity_description: Update the central tracking system status to "Documentation Complete" and trigger the Client Relations Team to initiate physical/digital handover.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm the Client Relations Team has acknowledged receipt of the Approved TDP.
process_activities.risk_considerations: The physical product is ready, but delivery stalls because the paperwork was never handed over.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Delivery & Handover Notice" and coordinate a delivery date/time with the client's receiving department.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Confirm the client has the necessary lifting equipment (e.g., forklift/hoist) available on-site for unloading.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Delivery vehicle arriving at a site unequipped to unload the heavy physical structure.

process_activities.activity_description: Secure an appropriate transport vehicle (e.g., standard pickup or flatbed) based on the structural dimensions and weight.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the vehicle payload capacity exceeds the final As-Built weight of the delivered equipment.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Transport vehicle failure or legal violations due to overloading.

process_activities.activity_description: Prepare the physical structure for transport by securing any movable clamps, applying protective wrap to precision datums, and loading it onto the transport vehicle.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure ratchet straps do not apply crushing force to delicate precision locating pins.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Irreversible dimensional damage or scratching of precision surfaces during transit.

process_activities.activity_description: Print physical copies of the Approved Client TDP (User Manual, Maintenance Guide, QA Certificates) and place them in a weatherproof document pouch attached to the shipment.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify all pages are printed clearly and bound professionally before dispatch.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client receiving damaged, unreadable, or missing operational manuals.

process_activities.activity_description: Arrive at the client site, supervise the safe unloading of the equipment, and move it to the designated operational footprint (e.g., workshop floor).
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the equipment is placed on a stable, level surface as defined in the initial site requirements.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Equipment instability due to placement on an uneven or unprepared workshop floor.

process_activities.activity_description: Perform a post-transit visual inspection and basic leveling/alignment using standard hand tools to ensure the structure survived transport intact.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Re-verify the primary datum plane squareness before handing it over to the client operators.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Handing over equipment that was subtly warped or knocked out of tolerance during the drive.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a physical walkthrough with the client's operators, demonstrating how to load a workpiece, engage clamps, and safely operate the equipment.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Force the client operators to physically perform the loading/clamping sequence themselves while you observe.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Operators nodding in agreement but failing to actually understand the safe handling procedures.

process_activities.activity_description: Present the formal "Final Handover Certificate" to the client's authorized representative for signature, legally transferring ownership and liability.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the signatory is a legally authorized client representative, not just a junior workshop floor technician.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Disputed project closure because the wrong person signed the acceptance forms.

process_activities.activity_description: Return the Signed Handover Certificate to the office, scan it, and upload it to the central database.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the scan is high-resolution and the signatures are clearly legible.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Loss of the physical paper leaving the company with no proof of delivery.

process_activities.activity_description: Trigger a "Project Delivered" notification to the Finance Team to immediately issue the final milestone invoice for the remaining contract balance.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Finance must issue the final invoice within 24 hours of receiving the handover authorization.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Delayed revenue collection causing unnecessary cash flow strain at the end of the project.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive the "Digitized Handover Record" and contact the client to schedule formal operator training sessions.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Ensure the scheduled training aligns with the shift patterns of the actual floor operators, not just management.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Training the wrong personnel, leaving actual machine operators uninstructed.

process_activities.activity_description: Extract the "Safe Handling and Operation" guidelines from the User Manual to structure a targeted, hands-on training module.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the syllabus directly addresses all Human System constraints identified in the Pr5 Specification.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Omitting critical safety or ergonomic warnings during training preparation.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct on-site, hands-on training with the client's operators, demonstrating loading, clamping, safe operation, and basic maintenance.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Force each operator to independently perform a full operational cycle while being observed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Operators passively watching but failing to grasp the tactile operational requirements.

process_activities.activity_description: Administer a brief verbal or practical competency check and have all trained operators sign the attendance log.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Only grant certification to operators who successfully complete the practical demonstration without critical errors.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unqualified operators damaging the equipment or injuring themselves post-handover.

process_activities.activity_description: Activate the formal 7 to 30-day "Post-Delivery Support" window in the tracking system, starting from the date of the signed Handover Certificate.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Set automated system alerts for the expiration date of the support window.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Providing indefinite free support due to a lack of formal boundary tracking.

process_activities.activity_description: Receive, log, and route any client feedback, minor adjustments, or operational questions that arise during the active support window.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Categorize requests strictly: verify if the issue is a genuine defect vs. a request for a new (out-of-scope) feature.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Executing out-of-scope engineering changes under the guise of "post-delivery support".

process_activities.activity_description: Schedule and conduct a final operational audit near the end of the support window to verify the equipment is performing optimally under standard production loads.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Cross-reference operator feedback against the original "Wishes and Fixed Requirements" to measure ultimate project success.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assuming the equipment is functioning perfectly just because the client hasn't called to complain.

process_activities.activity_description: Formally close the active support ticket and notify the client that standard warranty/maintenance contract terms now apply.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Offer a long-term preventative maintenance contract to the client concurrently with the closure notice.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Losing an opportunity for recurring revenue via maintenance contracts.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct a final sweep to ensure all logs (training, audit, support) are archived, and permanently lock the project ledger.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Verify the Finance Team confirms the final invoice has been fully paid and reconciled before locking the ledger.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Closing a project ledger when outstanding debt remains uncollected.

process_activities.activity_description: Identify project innovations and document client input vs IMHOGEN output per category using the Content Feed Tool.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Every content entry must be tied to a valid Project Request ID and have verified technical accuracy.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Releasing proprietary client data or unverified claims without clearance.

process_activities.activity_description: Document the process story milestones with plain-language descriptions and supporting QMS evidence references.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Each milestone must reference at least one QMS quality proof document.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Process story without evidence reduces credibility of marketing claims.

process_activities.activity_description: Map distribution channels and define call-to-action links ensuring every channel routes back to the IMHOGEN inquiry form.
process_activities.management_policy: Channel Mapping and Call to Action Policy
process_activities.quality_control_point: All channels must include the mandatory inquiry link before the feed is submitted.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Content distributed without CTA breaks the QMS lead-generation cycle.

process_activities.activity_description: R&D Lead certifies technical accuracy and client confidentiality clearance; marketing hub formally acknowledges receipt.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Both R&D Lead signature and Marketing Hub acknowledgment must be recorded before any external release.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unsigned content transmitted to marketing creates liability and audit gaps.

process_activities.activity_description: Define campaign theme and key message translating engineering outputs into client-facing benefit statements.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Campaign theme must reflect IMHOGEN engineering identity and be documented before any creative production begins.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Vague or off-brand campaign theme produces content that misrepresents the company.

process_activities.activity_description: List technical features with client-facing benefit statements and source document references for copywriting.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Every technical claim must cite a source document before the brief is issued.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unverified technical claims in marketing copy create reputational and legal exposure.

process_activities.activity_description: Specify mandatory CTA link, button text, and hub creative instructions; issue completed brief to marketing hub for execution.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Mandatory inquiry link must be present in every asset before brief is approved for production.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Assets without CTA break the lead-generation loop and waste marketing spend.

process_activities.activity_description: Catalogue all image assets with asset name, type, file path, and view description in the Visual Content Manifest.
process_activities.quality_control_point: All image assets must have a confirmed file link before being marked marketing-ready.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Broken or missing file links block campaign production.

process_activities.activity_description: Log all video and motion assets with duration, file path, and marketing purpose.
process_activities.quality_control_point: No video asset may be released without a confirmed file link and documented marketing purpose.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unverified video assets may contain safety violations or expose proprietary data.

process_activities.activity_description: Conduct brand compliance check: verify IMHOGEN watermark is applied and no safety violations are visible in any asset.
process_activities.quality_control_point: Watermark confirmed and zero safety violations present before assets are cleared for distribution.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unmarked or non-compliant assets published externally damage brand integrity and create liability.

process_activities.activity_description: R&D Lead authorizes visual assets for public distribution and records signature with approval date.
process_activities.quality_control_point: R&D Lead signature and date must be recorded in the manifest before any asset is released externally.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Unauthorized asset distribution exposes confidential client information.

process_activities.activity_description: Marketing hub compiles all approved MDS documents into a campaign package ready for publication.
process_activities.quality_control_point: All three MDS documents must be in approved state before hub synchronization can proceed.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Publishing an incomplete campaign package damages professional credibility.

process_activities.activity_description: PM reviews the final campaign package and provides confidentiality clearance signature on MDS.1.4 before public release.
process_activities.quality_control_point: PM must confirm no proprietary client information is present before final release approval.
process_activities.risk_considerations: Client confidentiality breach through marketing content creates legal liability and loss of trust.
